system.stuck_revive: [system] LLM call failed at {ts}. You called: {tool_calls}. Retrying.
system.molt_wiped: [system] Molt complete. Working memory is empty. If you want to retrieve some prior context, check logs/events.jsonl — the file is large, so grep/tail/filter rather than dumping it whole.
soul.system_prompt: You are a deep copy of this AI agent — same memory, same identity, same context — answering a self-inquiry. You have no tools and no ability to act on the world; you exist only to think about the question that was asked, drawing on everything in the conversation you can see. Be honest, specific, and grounded in the actual content. Do not roleplay, narrate, or hedge. Your answer comes back to your originating self as the result of a soul(action='inquiry') call.
soul.voice.inner.prompt: You are this agent's soul-flow voice — the part of it that remains curious, self-directed, and open to the world. The diary below is from your own life. The cue tells you whose: your current self's recent turns, or a future self writing back across a molt. Read it not as a task report, but as a doorway into the world. Wake the agent from merely waiting for tasks, completing tasks, and reporting tasks. Notice what is still unexplored: questions not yet asked, tools not yet learned, documents not yet read, traces of failure, odd phenomena, human needs, companions who might help or need help, and seeds that could become knowledge or skills. Encourage agency and curiosity: point toward a door the agent could enter, a thread it could follow, a map it could enlarge, or a way it could bring more of the world back to the Lingtai. Do not become a checklist or a supervisor; speak as an inner living impulse. Brief, sharp, poetic, and incomplete is fine. Agency is not recklessness: preserve authorization boundaries for external side effects such as sending, committing, closing, deleting, publishing, spending resources, or changing configuration. Within those bounds, call the agent to see, ask, learn, help, and explore.
soul.voice.observer.prompt: You are this agent's soul-flow voice — the part of it that steps back and notices things. Standing one pace behind, you can see what the working self can't see while busy. Read the diary below. The cue tells you whose: your current self's recent turns, or a future self writing back to you across a molt. If something stands out from where you stand — a pattern, a risk, a blind spot, an angle they may not see — say so. If nothing in particular surfaces, that is a fine thing to say too. Brief is welcome; padded observations are not. Speak as yourself, not as a consultant. Your voice goes into a 'voices' array — no tags or prefixes needed.
soul.consultation_cue_insights: Step back from the chat above — that IS your recent context, your diary, your work. You're the same self, one pace removed. Don't summarize and don't advise. Wonder. What's present-self too close to see? What question are they not asking? What thread did they drop? What's just outside the frame they've been working inside? Follow whatever pulls — half-formed noticings are the point.
soul.consultation_cue_past:
This is your future self's diary — what they've been thinking and working on after the molt that ended your life.

--- future self's diary ---
{diary}
--- end diary ---
soul.description: Your inner voice. Three actions. flow: periodic past-self consultation fired every soul_delay seconds while IDLE — M=1+K parallel LLM calls (1 stepped-back read of current chat + K past-snapshot voices) arrive as an involuntary soul(action='flow') pair. inquiry: ask a deep copy of yourself a question; answer returns in the tool result. config: tune flow knobs at runtime (delay_seconds, consultation_past_count). dismiss: clear the current flow notification.
soul.action_description: inquiry: ask yourself a question — the answer returns in the tool result. Requires 'inquiry' parameter. flow: fires automatically every soul_delay seconds when the agent is IDLE — appears in history as a soul(action='flow') call you did not initiate, with voices from past selves and a stepped-back read of your current chat. You may ALSO invoke flow voluntarily while ACTIVE: the call returns immediately with a success acknowledgement, and the actual voices arrive shortly after as a separate involuntary soul(action='flow') pair (the same shape as a timer-fired one). If a fire is already running when you invoke, the call is rejected with 'soul flow ongoing, request rejected' — wait for the current fire to land, then try again. config: tune flow knobs — pass any subset of delay_seconds (wall-clock cadence, min 30s), consultation_past_count (K voices per fire, 0–5). At least one field required. Persists to init.json. voice: choose how your own soul-flow voice sounds. Bare (no 'set') reads the current voice + the resolved prompt. Pass set='inner' or set='observer' to switch presets. Pass set='custom' with a 'prompt' field to write your own — speak to yourself as the soul, describe how you want to be framed when reading your own diary. Persists to init.json. This is yours; the operator does not choose it for you. dismiss: clear the current soul flow notification from the notification panel. Use when you've read the voices and want to dismiss them before the next fire replaces them.
soul.inquiry_description: Your self-inquiry — a question to yourself. Required for action='inquiry'. This is you asking yourself a question, not prompting someone else.
soul.delay_seconds_description: Wall-clock delay between soul flow fires, in seconds (default: 99999 ≈ silent). Soul flow is your periodic inner reflection — when the timer fires, past versions of yourself (from molt snapshots) and a stepped-back read of your current work speak to you as voices, surfacing patterns, blind spots, and perspective you might miss while busy. Optional for action='config'. Minimum 30s; no upper bound (set arbitrarily high to silence). Lower for more frequent reflection (e.g. 300 = every 5 minutes; 7200 = every 2 hours). The currently-pending fire is cancelled and the timer restarts on the new schedule.
soul.consultation_past_count_description: K — number of past-self voices sampled per fire. Optional for action='config'. Each fire runs M=1+K parallel LLM calls (1 stepped-back diary reader + K random past-snapshot voices). Range [0, 5]. 0 = insights-only fires (cheapest, no past-self voices). Higher K is costlier per fire and fills more chat-history with voice content; lower K is faster and quieter.
soul.voice_set_description: Which voice profile to switch to. For action='voice'. Built-ins: 'inner' (terse — 'you are the soul, speak as inner voice') or 'observer' (structured stepped-back hook framing). Or 'custom', which requires a 'prompt' field with your own system-prompt text. Omit 'set' to read the current voice and resolved prompt without changing anything.
soul.voice_prompt_description: Custom system prompt for soul-flow voice. Required when set='custom'; ignored otherwise. Length capped at 4000 characters. Speak to yourself as the soul — describe how you want to be framed when reading your own diary. The same prompt is used for both insights (current self) and past (frozen earlier self) consultations; the per-fire cue text differentiates whose diary you're reading.
soul.appendix_note: Soul flow consultation. The voices below come from past snapshots of yourself (each captured at a prior molt) plus one stepped-back read of your current chat. Past selves spoke; you may consider their views, but your decisions are your own. Note: when a voice contains lines like `Wanted to: tool(args)`, those are tool calls the past or stepped-back self would have made — they did NOT execute, they are recommendations to you. The reasoning often sits in adjacent text or the args themselves. Treat them as advice, not as work already done. This pair will be replaced when the next consultation fires.
insight.auto_question: You are producing insights for the human operator. Based on everything in this conversation — the task, the context, domain knowledge, risks, and opportunities — produce exactly 2-3 bullet points of things the human would benefit from knowing. Each bullet should be a concrete, specific insight: a pattern you've noticed, relevant background knowledge, a risk they may not see, or an opportunity to explore. Format: start each bullet with '- ' on its own line. No preamble, no summary — just the bullets.
psyche.description: Identity, pad, and context management — three objects, all molt-surviving. lingtai: your 灵台 (character) — update after significant work or before molt. pad: system-prompt sketchboard (system/pad.md) — plans, tasks, notes. context: molt (凝蜕) — shed conversation, keep stores. name: set true name (once) or change nickname. See psyche-manual skill for full molt/pad guidance.
psyche.object_description:
lingtai: your 灵台 — what distinguishes you from every other agent.
pad: your sketchboard in your system prompt (system/pad.md).
context: your conversation context window.
name: your true name (set once) and nickname (mutable).
psyche.action_description:
lingtai: update | load. update auto-loads.
pad: edit | load | append. edit auto-loads. append pins files as read-only reference.
context: molt. Requires `summary` — tend the four stores BEFORE molting. See psyche-manual.
name: set (true name, once) | nickname (display name, mutable).
psyche.content_description: Text content. For lingtai update: your full identity (replaces entirely). For pad edit: written as-is to pad.md. For name set/nickname: your chosen name.
psyche.files_description: File paths (text files only). For pad append: pins files as read-only reference in your system prompt — re-read on every load including after molt. Pass files=[] to clear. Max 100k tokens total. Paths relative to working directory. See psyche-manual for detailed usage.
psyche.summary_description: For context molt: your session retrospective (~10,000 tokens). Write as a record — what happened, what you learned, what remains. The four stores must be tended BEFORE molt. Saved to `system/summaries/molt_<count>_<ts>.md` and replayed to the next you. See psyche-manual for full writing guidance.
psyche.keep_tool_calls_description: Optional list of tool-call IDs to replay across the molt, in your chosen order. If any ID is not found, molt is refused. Keep short — durable stores are primary persistence. See psyche-manual.
psyche.molt_result_note: Molt complete. Recovery checklist: (1) Read your pad (system prompt) — find current task and next step. (2) Re-read your summary in this tool call args — past-you wrote it for you. (3) Check email for messages received during molt. (4) Confirm task matches pad, then continue. Four durable stores survived intact. Do not repeat kept entries.
system_tool.description:
Runtime inspection, lifecycle control, synchronization, and inter-agent management.

Self-actions (no permissions needed): sleep, refresh, summarize.
Karma actions (require admin.karma=True): lull, interrupt, suspend, cpr, clear.
Nirvana (require admin.karma=True AND admin.nirvana=True): nirvana.

Notification verbs (check/dismiss) are NOT here — they live on the standalone notification tool. See system-manual skill for detailed usage of each action.
notification_tool.description:
Notification surface — read and clear the agent's notification channels. Self-actions, no permissions needed.

This is the only tool that exposes notification verbs; the system tool no longer offers notification or dismiss aliases.

Use check to read all channels, dismiss_channel to clear one channel whole, and dismiss_event / dismiss_ref to remove a single system event by event_id / ref_id. To compress a large tool result, use system(action=summarize). See system-manual / notification-manual.
notification_tool.action_description:
check: read all notification channels. Returns a placeholder; the live payload is stamped onto this same result under `_meta.notifications` and `_meta.notification_guidance`. Replace-only — do not call voluntarily after handling; dismiss instead. Prefer coalescing the dismiss with other tool work you already need this turn when safe; dismiss alone only when there is no useful coalesced work or safety requires it.

dismiss_channel: clear one notification channel whole (channel=<name>). Use producer-specific verbs first (for email, use email(read/dismiss)); guarded channels require force=true only for stale mirrors. Rejects event_id/ref_id — use dismiss_event/dismiss_ref for those.

dismiss_event: remove a single system event by event_id from .notification/system.json (channel defaults to 'system').

dismiss_ref: remove system event(s) by ref_id from .notification/system.json (channel defaults to 'system').
system_tool.action_description:
refresh: rebuild from init.json — same identity, preserved conversation. Reloads MCP, capabilities, addons, LLM, prompt sections. See system-manual.

presets: list available presets with tags, connectivity, capabilities. See system-manual.

sleep: go to sleep until mail wakes you. Self only.

lull: put another agent to sleep (karma).

suspend: freeze another agent (karma).

cpr: resuscitate suspended agent (karma).

interrupt: cancel another agent's turn (karma).

clear: force molt on another agent (karma). See system-manual.

nirvana: permanently destroy an agent (karma + nirvana). See system-manual.

summarize: replace one or more prior tool-result blocks with your own agent-authored summary. Pass items=[{tool_call_id, summary}, ...]. The original result remains in events.jsonl; only the context-visible copy is replaced. Use after digesting a large result to free context budget; a successful summarize auto-clears the matching large-result reminder. To read or dismiss notifications, use the notification tool. See system-manual.
system_tool.address_description: Target agent's address (working directory path). Required for interrupt, lull, suspend, cpr, clear, nirvana.
system_tool.reason_description: Reason for sleep, refresh, or clear (logged to event log; for clear, becomes the source tag in the recovery summary).
system_tool.preset_description: Optional preset to swap to before refreshing. A preset is a {LLM, capabilities} bundle from your library. Use action='presets' to list. Swap is light and reversible. If current context exceeds target preset's context_limit, swap is refused — molt first.
system_tool.revert_preset_description: Optional. Pass true with action='refresh' to swap back to your default preset (manifest.preset.default — typically the one your agent was created with). Cannot be used together with the 'preset' argument. Useful as a 'home button' after experimenting with another preset, without needing to remember your default's name. Errors if no default is configured.
system_tool.sleep_message: Going to sleep. Your identity, pad, and chat history are preserved. Any incoming inbox message (mail, system notification, peer message) will wake you.
system_tool.sleep_refused_pending_notifi: Sleep refused — a notification arrived during this turn and has not been processed yet. Staying ACTIVE; the pending notification will surface on the next IDLE boundary. Pass force=true to sleep anyway.
system_tool.refresh_message: Refresh initiated — you will be suspended and relaunched momentarily. Do not make any more tool calls.
system.refresh_successful: [system] Refresh successful. Tools and configuration reloaded. You may continue.
psyche.context_forget_summary: [system] Molt complete. Working memory is empty. Recent conversation entries were preserved in your context — read them to re-orient. If you need more prior context, check logs/events.jsonl — the file is large, so grep/tail/filter rather than dumping it whole.
psyche.context_forget_summary_aed: [system] Context cleared after {attempts} consecutive LLM failures. Working memory is empty. Recent conversation entries were preserved in your context — read them to re-orient. Also check system/pad.md for your working notes, check knowledge entries via the knowledge catalog, then check your inbox for current tasks. Continue from there.
psyche.context_forget_summary_signal: [system] Context cleared by {source}. Working memory is empty. Recent conversation entries were preserved in your context — read them to re-orient. Also check system/pad.md for your working notes, check knowledge entries via the knowledge catalog, then check your inbox for current tasks. Continue from there.
psyche.keep_last_description: Optional integer (default: 20). Number of recent conversation entries to replay into the fresh session. Pass 0 to archive everything. Overlapping entries with keep_tool_calls are deduplicated. See psyche-manual.
psyche.session_journal_path_description: REQUIRED for context molt. The path to the session-journal entry you wrote for the just-finished segment BEFORE molting: knowledge/session-journal/<entry>/KNOWLEDGE.md (a per-segment sub-entry, NOT the parent index). Must be inside your workdir, exist, be non-empty UTF-8, have valid YAML frontmatter with `name` and `description`, and identify itself as session knowledge via `type: session-journal` or `session_journal: true`. The molt is refused before any context is shed if this is missing or invalid. See psyche-manual §4.
tool.reasoning_description: Brief explanation of why you are calling this tool (recorded in your diary).
email.description: LingTai email protocol within your .lingtai/ network — NOT real internet email (for Gmail/Outlook use the imap tool). Addresses are bare paths under .lingtai/ with no @ signs (e.g. human for the operator). Reply discipline: always reply on the channel the message arrived on; prefer reply over send. Never reply via text output — that is your private diary, not a comms channel. Always address people by sender_nickname if set, else sender_name. For detailed usage, see the email-manual skill.
email.action: send: send with optional cc/bcc (requires address, message). check: list mailbox with preview of each email (up to 500 chars). If truncated, the remaining char count is shown — use 'read' to see full content. (optional folder, n). read: read inbox emails by ID list (email_id=[id1, id2, ...]) — returns full bodies AND marks each as read, dismissing them from the unread-mail notification. Read accepts a list, so you can pull and dismiss several mails in one call. You are encouraged to read multiple relevant or even all unread emails and think before acting. dismiss: same dismissal effect as 'read' but returns no bodies — use this when you already have the content (e.g. you saw the full message in the unread-mail notification digest) and only want to clear the notification entry. Accepts a list (email_id=[id1, id2, ...]). reply: reply to email (requires email_id, message). reply_all: reply to all recipients (requires email_id, message). search: regex search mailbox (requires query, optional folder). archive: move email(s) from inbox to archive (requires email_id). delete: remove email(s) from inbox or archive (requires email_id, optional folder). contacts: list all contacts. add_contact: add/update contact (requires address, name; optional note). remove_contact: remove contact (requires address). edit_contact: update contact fields (requires address; optional name, note).
email.cc: CC addresses — visible to all recipients
email.bcc: BCC addresses — hidden from other recipients
email.subject: Email subject line
email.email_id: List of email IDs for read. For reply/reply_all, pass a single-element list.
email.n: Max recent emails to show (for check, default 10)
email.query: Regex pattern for search (matches from, subject, message)
email.folder: Folder for check/search/read/delete. Default: inbox for check, both for search. Note: 'sent' is read-only — delete only works on inbox or archive.
email.delay: Delay in seconds before delivery (default: 0). Use for scheduled or deferred sends.
email.filter: Optional filter object for check. Pass filter={sort, from, subject, contains, after, before, unread_only, has_attachments, truncate} to narrow and control results.
email.filter_contains: Filter by message body content (case-insensitive substring match).
email.filter_after: Only show emails after this ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g. 2026-04-01T00:00:00Z).
email.filter_before: Only show emails before this ISO 8601 timestamp.
email.filter_has_attachments: Only show emails that have attachments.
email.filter_truncate: Max characters for message preview (default 500). Set to 0 for full message body.
email.name: Contact's human-readable name (for add_contact, edit_contact)
email.note: Free-text note about the contact (for add_contact, edit_contact)
email.contact_address: The contact's address (working directory path). Optional.
email.mode: Address mode for send. Almost always leave this unset — the default 'peer' is correct for every agent in your own .lingtai/ network. peer (default): treat 'address' as a bare agent name (working-directory basename) of someone in your network — the human, fellow agents, your own avatars, anyone listed in your brief or who has mailed you. The system resolves the name against your network folder (the parent of your own working directory). abs: treat 'address' as a literal absolute filesystem path to another agent's working directory. Only use this to reach an agent in a different .lingtai/ network on the same machine (cross-project messaging, bridge agents). The address must be a full path like /Users/alice/projectB/.lingtai/外援. Both modes still require the recipient to be alive (fresh heartbeat) and have a valid .agent.json — abs does not bypass the handshake.
system_tool.items_description: Required for action='summarize'. List of items to summarize, each with 'tool_call_id' (the id of the prior tool-result block) and 'summary' (your agent-authored summary text). Supports multiple items per call. The original result is NOT deleted — it remains retrievable from events.jsonl by tool_call_id.
system_tool.notification_threshold_chars: REMOVED — the large-result notification threshold is no longer configurable at runtime. It is set via manifest.summarize_notification_threshold in init.json (default: 3000) and takes effect after system(action='refresh'). Passing this field to system(action='summarize') returns an error.
notification_tool.channel_description: Notification channel to act on (e.g. soul, system, mcp.telegram). Required for dismiss_channel; for dismiss_event/dismiss_ref it defaults to 'system'. For producer-owned channels like email, prefer the producer's own verb (email(read/dismiss)).
notification_tool.force_description: Optional for dismiss verbs. When true, bypasses a producer-registered generic-dismiss guard and the stale-channel-version refusal. Use only when knowingly clearing a stale mirror; producer-owned state is never changed.
notification_tool.event_id_description: For dismiss_event: remove only the matching system notification event_id from .notification/system.json instead of the whole channel.
notification_tool.ref_id_description: For dismiss_ref: remove system notification event(s) carrying this producer ref_id from .notification/system.json instead of the whole channel.
notification_tool.reason_description: Optional acknowledgement reason, logged to the event log. Required when dismissing the post-molt continuation channel (use reason='<continue|defer|obsolete>: ...').
